items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to discuss Mountain View's planned infrastructure and capital projects for the next fiscal year. This is worth following if you care about what public works, facilities, or development projects the city is prioritizing with its budget.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission is introducing a new ordinance to amend tenant protections in the city. This could affect renter rights and landlord-tenant relationships for Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: City releases Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, which outlines improvements to library services and physical spaces. This plan affects where and how residents access library resources across the city.

items.headline: Youth services programs report on summer initiatives and intervention work
items.summary: The City Council reviewed annual reports from youth programs including the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services on April 9. These programs provide summer activities and support services for San José young people.

items.headline: Historic downtown building seeks bonus floor space for rehabilitation
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting to be elevated from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposed eight-story building with 167 apartments and commercial space would replace service commercial properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road, requiring rezoning. The project includes 28 below-market-rate units and approximately 1,400 square feet of commercial space.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street project hearing postponed, new date TBD
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a development project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled. The project will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed, currently expected for April 22, 2026 or later.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what this construction project entails and where it will take place in Los Gatos.
